varieties.name: Australian Bruce
varieties.origin: Australia (developed from local selection)
varieties.traits: Long fruits (12cm), transforms from green to vibrant red at maturity, crispy texture, attractive appearance when full of pods
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropping, prolific producer, plant height 75cm

varieties.name: Australian Broome
varieties.origin: Broome, Western Australia (named after the town)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruits, untamed and rugged flavor profile, compact plant (50-60cm in pots)
varieties.yield_notes: Bountiful yields, resilient plants
varieties.availability: The Hippy Seed Company, specialty Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Cayenne (Long Red & Green)
varieties.origin: Cayenne, French Guiana (South America). Heirloom variety, first sold by seed traders in 1883
varieties.traits: Long (10-15cm), thin, wrinkled fruits. Green cayenne provides mild introduction to heat; red cayenne offers sweet notes with medium heat
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, continuous harvest through season

varieties.name: Jalapeño
varieties.origin: Mexico (Jalapa, Veracruz)
varieties.traits: Cylindrical, thick-fleshed fruits (7-10cm long), rounded end, green maturing to red
varieties.yield_notes: Very popular, productive plants with chunky fruits
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates Markets, Queensland nurseries (Mudbrick Herb Cottage, Infusion Chilli Farms Redland Bay), seed companies, widely available in Australian garden centers

varieties.name: Habanero (Orange & Red)
varieties.origin: Amazon region/Caribbean, name from Havana, Cuba
varieties.traits: Small (5cm), lantern-shaped, distinctive campanulate shape, starts green ripening to orange/yellow or red
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer with compact plants, many fruits per bush

varieties.name: Serrano
varieties.origin: Mexican mountain regions (Puebla, Hidalgo)
varieties.traits: Bullet-shaped fruits (6cm long), slender like jalapeño but with rounded end, thick flesh, bright green to red
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, continuous harvest

varieties.name: Bird's Eye / Thai Chilli
varieties.origin: Cambodia, Vietnam, Thailand, Philippines; introduced to region by Portuguese in 16th-17th centuries
varieties.traits: Small (1-2 inches/2.5-5cm), thin, pointed fruits growing upright; green, yellow, orange to red; highly ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive with many small peppers per bush, continuous harvest
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates Markets, Queensland nurseries, widely available at humid subtropical climates garden centers, Asian grocery stores for fresh produce

varieties.name: Poblano / Ancho
varieties.origin: Puebla, Mexico (named after the state). Heirloom variety
varieties.traits: Large (4-5 inches/10-12cm), broad, heart-shaped pepper with thick walls; dark glossy green maturing to deep reddish-brown; when dried becomes 'Ancho chile'
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, moderate yields
varieties.availability: Queensland specialty nurseries, seed suppliers (heirloom collections, Succeed Heirlooms), some humid subtropical climates garden centers

varieties.name: Anaheim / New Mexico
varieties.origin: New Mexico, USA; named after Anaheim, California where Emilio Ortega brought seeds in late 1800s. Heirloom variety
varieties.traits: Long (6-10 inches/15-25cm), slender, curved fruits; bright green maturing to vibrant red; thick walls; when dried called 'California chile'
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, good yields
varieties.availability: Queensland specialty nurseries, seed companies (heirloom seed suppliers, Survival Seeds), some humid subtropical climates garden centers

varieties.name: Carolina Reaper
varieties.origin: South Carolina, USA (developed by Ed Currie). Hybrid cross between ghost pepper and red habanero
varieties.traits: Small, bumpy, tail-like point, irregular shape; ripens from green to bright red
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield, requires long growing season
varieties.availability: Specialty Queensland nurseries (Exotic Chillies, Infusion Chilli Farms Redland Bay), AustChilli Bundaberg, seed suppliers (The Hippy Seed Company, Survival Seeds, Chillibird)

varieties.name: Jwala Mukhi
varieties.origin: Developed by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), Vellanikkara Horticultural College
varieties.traits: Can be grown in pots as ornamental plant; non-contagious; medium-length fruits
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, thick-skinned variety
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, KAU-affiliated nurseries, tropical monsoon climates agricultural departments

varieties.name: Jwala Sakhi
varieties.origin: Developed by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), College of Agriculture, Vellayani
varieties.traits: Light green fruit color; short crop duration 4-5 months
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 20 t/ha green chilli
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, KAU Vellayani campus, tropical monsoon climates agricultural departments, southern tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Ujjwala
varieties.origin: Developed by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), College of Horticulture, Vellanikkara
varieties.traits: Dark green fruits turning dark brown on ripening; 6cm length; borne in bunches
varieties.yield_notes: Good yield, suitable for both green chilli and drying

varieties.name: Anugraha
varieties.origin: Developed by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), College of Horticulture, Vellanikkara
varieties.traits: Medium stature plants with attractive long green fruits turning deep red; average fruit weight 3.6g
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, average yield 27 t/ha, early maturing

varieties.name: Vellayani Athulya
varieties.origin: Developed by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), College of Agriculture, Vellayani
varieties.traits: Shade-tolerant kanthari-type variety; small fruits similar to kanthari
varieties.yield_notes: 32 t/ha green chilli

varieties.name: Vellayani Samridhi
varieties.origin: Developed by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), College of Agriculture, Vellayani
varieties.traits: Shade-tolerant kanthari-type variety; short plant with high yield; sharp, shiny-ended fruit; thick skin
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, 27 t/ha green chilli

varieties.name: Vellayani Tejus
varieties.origin: Developed by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), College of Agriculture, Vellayani
varieties.traits: Newer variety from Vellayani program
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University Vellayani, tropical monsoon climates agricultural departments

varieties.name: Pant C-1
varieties.origin: Developed by Pantnagar Agricultural University, Uttarakhand
varieties.traits: Popular North Indian variety adapted to various conditions

varieties.name: K-2
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates development
varieties.traits: Erect plants with height of 90.2cm
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding variety
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates agricultural departments, tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Byadagi 🏆
varieties.origin: Byadagi town, Haveri district, Karnataka, India (neighboring state). GI-tagged product
varieties.traits: Long, wrinkled, deeply red colored; resembles paprika; thick-skinned; retains nutritional values for extended periods
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, widely cultivated in Karnataka
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates spice markets, South Indian agricultural stores, widely available in tropical monsoon climates due to regional cuisine importance

varieties.name: Guntur Sannam 🌏
varieties.origin: Guntur district, Andhra Pradesh, India (neighboring state). World's largest chilli trading hub
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, bright red; king of red chilli variants in India; known worldwide
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, accounts for nearly 30% of India's red chilli exports

varieties.name: Kashmiri Chilli 🏆
varieties.origin: Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh, North India
varieties.traits: Fleshy, long, deep red color; larger fruits; most extensively used in Indian households
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields from cool mountain climate
